news.headline: Under fire, Kharkiv is already building for a peaceful tomorrow

news.headline: Borrowed boots, borrowed joy: Football thrives in Gaza camps
news.summary: Matches organised by a former professional player are providing a brief respite from the harsh reality of life for the thousands living in overcrowded tents, schools or damaged buildings in the shattered Occupied Palestinian Territory of Gaza.

news.headline: World News in Brief: Drone strike in Romania condemned, Lebanon and South Sudan updates
news.summary: The UN Secretary-General has expressed alarm after a drone struck a residential building in the Romanian city of Galați overnight into Friday, reportedly injuring two people.

news.headline: Conflict-related sexual violence cases more than doubled in 2025, UN warns
news.summary: Nearly 10,000 cases of conflict-related sexual violence were recorded worldwide last year – more than double the previous year's figure – as rape, sexual slavery and abduction were deployed as weapons of war across Africa, the Middle East, Europe and the Caribbean.

news.headline: UN honours blue helmets and calls for renewed investment in peace
news.summary: Peacekeepers are serving in a dangerous era of mounting global tensions and tight resources. UN chief António Guterres is now calling for greater political backing and reliable financial support for ‘blue helmets’ who put themselves in harm’s way.

news.headline: Gaza: 26 killed over Eid holiday, UN rights investigators report
news.summary: At least 26 Palestinians were killed in the Gaza Strip since Tuesday – the eve of one of the most important holidays in Islam – the UN human rights office, OHCHR, reported on Friday.

news.headline: Gaza children trapped ‘in an endless cycle of suffering’: UNICEF
news.summary: The dire conditions in the Gaza Strip are trapping children “in an endless cycle of suffering” and their heartbroken parents can only look on, an official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF said on Friday in a fresh appeal for greater humanitarian access to support families in the war-ravaged en

news.headline: ‘A disease you get when you care for someone’: WHO on the Ebola frontline
news.summary: Two weeks into the latest deadly Ebola outbreak, the World Health Organization (WHO) estimates there are now 906 suspected cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), including 223 suspected deaths.

news.headline: UN warns Ukraine war risks spiralling ‘out of control’
news.summary: The United Nations on Thursday warned of a dangerous escalation in the war in Ukraine after a wave of large-scale Russian strikes and threats of further attacks, with Secretary-General António Guterres saying “the death spiral must stop.”

news.headline: World News in Brief: Lebanon violence continues, Israel ‘severs ties’ with UN chief, Internal displacement in Haiti, warning to France over prison overcrowding
news.summary: The UN has expressed deep concern over escalating hostilities in Lebanon following intensified Israeli airstrikes in southern Beirut and across southern parts of the country.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting
news.summary: The UN&nbsp;Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Ukraine, with Secretary-General António Guterres telling ambassadors following what Kyiv describes as the most devastating attack on its capital to date, that “the time for peace is now.” In the early hours of 23-24 May, Russia laun

news.headline: WHO chief calls for ceasefire amid DR Congo Ebola outbreak
news.summary: The Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO) headed to the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) on Thursday as the country continues to combat a deadly resurgence of Ebola in its volatile eastern region where instability is rife.&nbsp;

news.headline: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
news.summary: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acut

news.headline: World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights
news.summary: The United Nations is raising alarm over continued drone attacks in Sudan’s Darfur region after multiple strikes reportedly killed civilians and intensified fears for communities already trapped by the brutal conflict between rival militaries.

news.headline: Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of  world order, warns UN chief
news.summary: The UN Charter is facing one of its gravest tests in decades, Secretary-General António Guterres told the Security Council on Tuesday, warning that wars, arms races, climate shocks and the erosion of international law are placing intense pressure on the multilateral system established to prevent a t

news.headline: Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro
news.summary: The World Food Programme (WFP) has deplored a Russian attack on a warehouse in Dnipro, Ukraine that destroyed “a significant quantity” of food aid destined for thousands in frontline areas.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Founding principles of the UN facing ‘profound strain’, Guterres warns
news.summary: The Security Council is meeting for a high-level debate on strengthening the UN-centred international system amid mounting pressure on multilateralism and deepening geopolitical divisions. Discussions are expected to focus on defending the UN Charter, reforming global governance and restoring confid

news.headline: Middle East update: Airstrikes and evacuation orders in Lebanon, aid restrictions in Gaza
news.summary: Intensified Israeli airstrikes overnight in Lebanon forced people to again flee their homes, while humanitarians in the Gaza Strip report continued restrictions in bringing aid into the enclave, the United Nations said on Tuesday.

news.headline: International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak
news.summary: As a deadly Ebola strain continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), with cases confirmed in neighbouring Uganda, the UN aviation agency is urging governments and flight operators to closely follow guidelines put in place following the COVID-19 pandemic.

news.headline: Ebola epidemic spreading rapidly and outpacing containment efforts
news.summary: There are more than 900 suspected cases of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and 220 suspected deaths, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), Tedros Ghebreyesus,&nbsp;said on Monday.

news.headline: U.S. conducts "self-defense" strikes, CENTCOM says, insists ceasefire still in place amid negotiations - CBS News

news.headline: Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’
news.summary: The overnight Russian attack on Ukraine, which centred on the capital, Kyiv, reportedly involved some 90 missiles, including a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile and 60 drones. The senior UN official in the country called for an end to civilian harm.

news.headline: Displaced in Gaza: From home comforts to the shattering horror of war
news.summary: In front of a simple tent in Gaza that offers no protection from the cold or the heat, in a crowded camp where tents lack privacy and basic services, Umm Ahmad&nbsp;sat down to speak to UN News about her life in Gaza before the war and what it has become now.&nbsp;

news.headline: Review of landmark nuclear treaty breaks up without consensus, raising arms race fears
news.summary: After four weeks of negotiations at UN Headquarters in New York, the 11th&nbsp;Review Conference&nbsp;of the nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) ended on Friday without consensus on a final declaration.

news.headline: From Kharkiv to the UK’s health service: Ukrainian doctor rebuilds her life – and helps others do the same
news.summary: On 23 February 2022, in her home city of Kharkiv, Dr Inna Soldatenko finished work, collected her daughter from school, cooked dinner and prepared a lecture for her students. The next morning, she awoke to explosions.

news.headline: Trump’s Taiwan Threats: US Arms Freeze, China Tensions, and Taiwan’s Military Future - Modern Diplomacy
news.summary: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiugFBVV95cUxQd2JkNUxVd2pJVzVoSVdSLW1jTUhtWGxNY0lNbjRDUXRpeWYwWVpVdE5Ia09ORlhQWm8tcUFQdHl4eUZJdEE0RXFzLVZadm1UTjRabndLbUpOMlhtSElIQmVfV0E0WkFjWGFaYmF6TlBDX014ZTAzSmY0cWwxWlI4YVZGUWNkLTBXVnlyVDZTb2M5OGM4cXNHYnBBY0d6bU4weWZ3QXprcU5fV0lQZU1iQ21XOEl5U1d

news.headline: Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk
news.summary: The United Nations voiced alarm on Friday over reports of an overnight attack on a vocational school and dormitory in the town of Starobilsk in Ukraine’s Luhansk region which killed and injured multiple civilians, including children.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official
news.summary: The Security Council is meeting at the request of Russia which on Friday accused Ukraine of targeting a student dormitory overnight in the occupied Luhansk region, reportedly killing six people – including children – and injuring dozens. Kyiv has denied targeting the civilian building, saying it had

news.headline: Gaza: Life-saving medicines blocked as killing continues, disease gains ground
news.summary: Dire conditions in Gaza marked by continuing violence, rodent infestations and the spread of infectious disease are being made worse by blockages of essential medical supplies, UN agencies warned on Friday.

news.headline: Ebola risk ‘very high’ in eastern DR Congo as UN intensifies response
news.summary: The UN is rushing emergency personnel, funding and supplies into eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) to counter the fast-growing Ebola outbreak spreading through conflict-ravaged provinces.

news.headline: Gaza risks ‘permanent’ state of limbo if transition plan stalls, Security Council hears
news.summary: As Gaza’s fragile ceasefire frays and humanitarian conditions deteriorate, a senior UN envoy warned the Security Council on Thursday that delays in implementing the Council-backed transition plan for the enclave will only increase suffering and undermine recovery.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Ambassadors debate future of Gaza amid stalled progress on lasting peace
news.summary: The Security Council is discussing the stalled progress in Gaza as the fragile ceasefire there limps on, and deteriorating conditions across the West Bank amid continued civilian casualties and mounting humanitarian needs. Discussions are likely to focus on who governs Gaza, and how - with no disarm

news.headline: UN agencies step up Ebola response in eastern DR Congo
news.summary: United Nations agencies have moved swiftly to support efforts to contain the latest Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), delivering emergency medical supplies, protective equipment and logistics support.

news.headline: Haitian children ‘paying the highest price’ amid surge in gang recruitment
news.summary: As gangs continue to “terrorise” communities in Haiti, children are the ones paying the highest price, the UN Special Representative for Children and Armed Conflict warned on Thursday.

news.headline: Guterres condemns Israeli move to militarise seized compound in occupied East Jerusalem
news.summary: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Wednesday strongly condemned Israel’s decision to establish military facilities at a seized UNRWA compound in occupied East Jerusalem, calling the move “wholly unacceptable”.

news.headline: Flak jackets and final goodbyes: Lebanon’s first responders under fire
news.summary: Before heading to strike sites in war-torn Lebanon, rescue workers and paramedics often say goodbye to one another – a ritual captured in widely shared videos reflecting the growing dangers faced by aid workers since hostilities between Hezbollah and Israel erupted on 2 March.

news.headline: Ebola outbreak: ‘Every epidemic begins in a community and ends in a community’
news.summary: Facing a fast-moving Ebola outbreak caused by a rare strain of the virus, the World Health Organization (WHO) is relying on a rapid, community-centred response to halt transmission in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, a senior official has told UN News.

news.headline: Ebola risk is high inside DR Congo but it’s no pandemic emergency: WHO
news.summary: The deadly Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda does not represent a global pandemic emergency, although the risk is high at a regional and national level, the UN health agency chief said on Wednesday.

news.headline: 30,000 people flee fresh violence in Haiti as hunger crisis deepens
news.summary: Around 30,000 people have had to flee their homes in the Haitian capital Port-au-Prince and surrounding areas following a new wave of violent attacks and atrocities perpetrated by armed gangs over the past ten days.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Concerns grow over nuclear safety after UAE drone strike
news.summary: The UN Security Council is meeting in emergency session in New York to discuss the situation in the Middle East following reports of a drone strike near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in the United Arab Emirates. IAEA Director General Rafael Mariano Grossi briefed members as concerns grow over nucl
